stories.body: The Republican-controlled US Senate voted 50-48 to approve a concurrent war powers resolution instructing President Donald Trump to halt military action in Iran or obtain congressional approval, marking the first time both chambers have backed such a measure since 1973, though it has no force of law and will not be sent to the president; the move followed growing Republican unease over the unpopular five-month-old war’s impact on fuel prices, saw four GOP senators join Democrats while one Democrat opposed it, and underscored intraparty divisions ahead of midterm elections.
stories.headline: Congress passes war powers measure for first time, rebuking Trump's war with Iran

stories.body: US Central Command said American forces destroyed Iranian coastal radar and surveillance posts at Goruk and Qeshm Island after shooting down four Iranian drones aimed at Strait of Hormuz shipping, an escalation that comes amid faltering talks over a war-ending interim deal, Iranian demands for sanctions relief and leverage over the strait, and continued regional violence involving Hezbollah, Israel and US forces despite ceasefire efforts.
stories.headline: US attacks Iranian coastal sites after Iran launches drones in latest flare-up

stories.body: Iranian officials reaffirmed backing for Hezbollah and demanded an Israeli withdrawal from southern Lebanon as a precondition for any US-Iran ceasefire deal, warning that Lebanon must be part of any agreement ending the four-month-old regional war and touting Hezbollah’s sacrifices while Tehran and Washington continue indirect negotiations over sanctions relief, Hormuz shipping access and Iran’s nuclear program.
stories.headline: Iran declares support for Hezbollah as wider peace deal with Washington wobbles

stories.body: Iranian media reported that Tehran is reviewing a proposed interim agreement with the United States to end their war and reopen the largely shuttered Strait of Hormuz, even as Israel maintains strikes in Lebanon, oil markets remain volatile, and Iran pushes for a limited deal granting access to oil revenues, sanctions waivers, port unblockage and continued leverage over key maritime chokepoints while both sides trade sporadic strikes despite an April ceasefire.
stories.headline: Iran studying deal to halt war as Hormuz stalemate persists

stories.body: Temperatures in southern England hit 34.6C and could reach 39C later this week, prompting rare red weather and health alerts, school closures, rail disruptions and water stress as climate change-driven heat domes also scorch France and Spain, leading officials such as UN Secretary-General António Guterres to warn that London is “cooking” and infrastructure built for a cooler climate is failing to keep pace.
stories.headline: Extreme UK heat leaves schools, hospitals and transport systems struggling to cope

stories.body: Senate Republicans pushed through a $70 billion Department of Homeland Security supplemental to sustain Donald Trump’s immigration enforcement surge, rejecting Democratic and some GOP attempts to kill a $1.8 billion “anti-weaponization” fund that critics call a slush fund for the president’s allies, while intraparty fights over the ballot-room ballroom Trump wants and the controversial fund exposed vulnerable Republicans ahead of the midterms.
stories.headline: US Senate passes $70 billion ICE funding but keeps Trump's 'anti-weaponization' fund alive

stories.body: In a 50-48 vote mirroring the House, the Senate passed a war powers resolution directing President Trump to end hostilities against Iran or get congressional authorization, with four Republicans joining most Democrats while one Democrat opposed, underscoring bipartisan fatigue with the February-launched war even as the administration negotiates a shaky ceasefire memorandum with Tehran amid continued Israeli strikes in Lebanon.
stories.headline: US Senate votes to halt Iran war, bucking Trump

stories.body: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s progressive slate won key Democratic congressional primaries, celebrating alongside candidates Brad Lander and Darializa Avila Chevalier as they unseated two incumbents in contests that drew enthusiastic supporters across the city and signaled growing influence for Mamdani’s left-wing network heading into November.
stories.headline: Mamdani-backed slate sweeps New York Democratic primaries, ousting two incumbents

stories.body: A POLITICO/Public First survey found 44% of US adults want election betting outlawed and majorities oppose wagers on wars or terrorism, highlighting public unease even as platforms like Kalshi and Polymarket scale up political markets now worth hundreds of millions, amid regulatory scrutiny following a CFTC court loss and insider-trading case and despite backers touting prediction markets as useful forecasting tools.
stories.headline: Poll: Americans sour on political betting even as markets boom

stories.body: Campaign finance records reviewed by POLITICO indicate Arizona Sen. Ruben Gallego repeatedly used his campaign and leadership PAC accounts to cover family trips to Miami, Chicago, Disneyland, Disney World and St. Barts, reimburse more than $18,000 in child care, and buy tickets to Super Bowl LVII for a high-dollar fundraiser with Eric Swalwell, drawing criticism that he treats donor funds like a personal slush fund even as Gallego says the spending complies with FEC rules.
stories.headline: Sen. Ruben Gallego used campaign cash for family travel and Super Bowl tickets, records show

stories.body: Spain’s Supreme Court sentenced former transport minister José Luis Ábalos to 24 years and ex-adviser Koldo García to nearly 20 for running a criminal organization that profited from early-pandemic mask contracts and public-company hires while also concluding they schemed to collect kickbacks from public-works awards, bolstering a broader National Court probe into alleged bid rigging.
stories.headline: Supreme Court ruling finds Ábalos and García orchestrated public-works rigging scheme

stories.body: Following the Supreme Court’s landmark Koldo case ruling, Spain’s anti-corruption prosecutors plan to pursue evidence implicating José Luis Ábalos’s former partner Jésica Rodríguez—who admitted living in a rent-free apartment and holding no-show public jobs—as well as other associates like ex-Adif chief Isabel Pardo de Vera and Joseba García, potentially expanding criminal charges as the wider investigation continues in the National Court.
stories.headline: Anti-corruption prosecutors eye Ábalos’s ex-partner after Supreme Court verdict

stories.body: Former prime minister Felipe González and Castilla-La Mancha leader Emiliano García-Page said the Supreme Court’s conviction of ex-minister Ábalos is as serious as the corruption scandal that brought down Mariano Rajoy, urging Pedro Sánchez to resign or call elections before he appears in Congress to answer for cases engulfing the PSOE and warning that democratic consequences are unavoidable.
stories.headline: Felipe González urges Sánchez to resign or call elections ahead of Congress grilling

stories.body: Madrid judge Juan Carlos Peinado summoned Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez’s wife Begoña Gómez to hand over her passport as part of precautionary measures imposed when he ordered her to stand trial for alleged influence peddling, business corruption, embezzlement and misappropriation, a ruling her defense team is appealing and which has triggered a judicial review of the judge’s claim that her police escorts could abet flight.
stories.headline: Judge orders Begoña Gómez to surrender passport as corruption case advances

stories.body: Barcelona’s new Guardia Urbana unit embedded with the provincial prosecutor’s office has processed 72 cases since February—51 already resolved—targeting repeat offenders in theft, robbery, gender violence, fraud, money laundering and protection of minors, with Mayor Jaume Collboni touting its rapid, surgical work while Chief Prosecutor Neus Pujal called the collaboration a success that helps prosecutors determine recidivism and gather key surveillance evidence.
stories.headline: Guardia Urbana–Fiscalía task force in Barcelona handles 72 repeat-offender cases in four months

stories.body: Twelve new video surveillance cameras focused on Barceloneta’s seafront, installed between Plaça del Mar and Carrer de Trelawny, went live for the Sant Joan festivities to deter crime and provide prosecutorial evidence while respecting beachgoers’ privacy, and Mayor Jaume Collboni said the city plans to add 500 cameras this term and 1,000 next term, including upcoming deployments along La Rambla, Plaça Reial and potentially Turó de la Rovira.
stories.headline: Barcelona activates new seafront surveillance cameras ahead of Sant Joan festivities

stories.body: Restaurateur Bruno Balbás said the Banco de Boquerones group will shutter Casa Leopoldo on June 28, two years after reviving the 1936-founded Raval institution, citing €324,000 in investments, weak midday business, changing dining habits toward faster, lower-quality meals, and economic headwinds despite strong weekend trade, while expressing hope someone else will eventually reopen the beloved venue.
stories.headline: Historic Casa Leopoldo in El Raval closes again, citing rising costs and shifting habits
